hero.title: Discover Marulanda: where the cloud forest touches the sky
hero.subtitle: Three hours from Manizales, in the heart of Caldas, there is a town where clouds sleep on the rooftops, wax palms reach 60 meters, and wool is still spun by hand. This is not tourism. This is coming home.
hero.cta: I want to live the adventure

destino.title: It's not a destination. It's a rhythm.
destino.p1: In the mountains of Caldas, at 3,200 meters above sea level, Marulanda defies the rush of the 21st century. It's a town where time is measured in skeins of wool and the only clock that matters is the sun hiding behind the wax palms.
destino.p2: There are no traffic lights here, but there is a sky that changes color every hour. Cobblestone streets rise and fall between wattle-and-daub houses with carved wooden eaves. The elderly sit in the square watching the afternoon go by, and the ambient sound is a mix of wind, birds, and looms.
destino.p3: Marulanda is not visited: it is lived. And from La Ruta de la Lana, we invite you to live it with all five senses wide open.

experiencias.heading: Experiences that stay with you
experiencias.subtitle: Each experience is designed so you leave with more than you came with.
experiencias.cta: I want to live this experience

whyUs.heading: We're not a travel agency. We're neighbors.
whyUs.body: La Ruta de la Lana was born in Marulanda, by people who love this land. We don't operate from an office in Bogotá: we live here, we know every trail, every weaving family, and every sunrise in the páramo. When you travel with us, you travel with someone who opens their home to you.

cards.description: Nobody knows the terrain like someone who has walked it since childhood.

cards.title: Regenerative tourism
cards.description: Every visit leaves a direct contribution to the community and the ecosystem.

ctaSection.title: Marulanda is waiting for you
ctaSection.body: Every trip with us directly supports farming families, protects the cloud forest, and keeps the region's textile tradition alive. This is not just any tourism: it's tourism that leaves a mark.

experiences.title: Walk Among the Clouds
experiences.subtitle: Cloud forests, biodiversity, and absolute silence
experiences.description: Step into one of the most magical ecosystems on the planet. Marulanda's cloud forests are reservoirs of biodiversity: wild orchids, giant bromeliads, endemic birds, and a silence broken only by the wind in the canopy. Guided hikes for all levels, from contemplative nature walks to high-mountain trekking.
experiences.longDescription: Marulanda's cloud forest is one of the most fascinating and fragile ecosystems in Colombia's Central mountain range. At over 2,800 meters above sea level, mist wraps around every tree and trail, creating a fairy-tale landscape where light filters through centuries-old mosses. Here live bird species found nowhere else on Earth, miniature orchids sprouting from lichen-covered trunks, and a calm so deep that it disconnects you from the world's noise entirely. This experience is led by local experts who grew up in these mountains and can read every sound, every track, and every shift in the wind.
includes: Round-trip transport from and to Marulanda
itinerary: Departure from Marulanda town at 8:00 a.m.
4x4 ride to the trailhead
Guided 2-4 hour hike through the cloud forest
Birdwatching and native flora identification
Farmstead lunch at a traditional farm
Return to Marulanda at sunset
experiences.tips: The best months to visit the cloud forest are December through March and July through August, when rains ease up and visibility is at its best.

experiences.title: Stand Before the Tallest Palm in the World
experiences.subtitle: Natural sanctuary of the Quindío wax palm
experiences.description: The Quindío wax palm — Colombia's national tree — finds one of its most impressive sanctuaries in Marulanda. Walk through entire valleys where these giants rise over 60 meters, flanked by frailejones and a horizon that takes your breath away.
experiences.longDescription: The wax palm sanctuary in the mountains of Marulanda is one of the best-kept secrets of Colombia's Coffee Region. Unlike the tourist valleys, here the palms rise solitary among pastures and hillsides, creating a landscape that looks like a 19th-century romantic painting. Each palm can live up to 200 years and reach 70 meters in height, making it the tallest palm in the world. Walking among them is a humbling experience: you feel tiny beside these ancient ones that have seen history pass by. The hike crosses páramo zones with frailejones, viewpoints overlooking the entire valley, and farms where the land is still worked as it was a hundred years ago.
includes: Local guide with palm ecosystem expertise
itinerary: Departure from Marulanda at 7:00 a.m. in a 4x4 vehicle
Ascent to the palm viewing area
3-4 hour interpretive hike among century-old palms
Visit to panoramic valley viewpoints
Lunch at a traditional farm with local products
Walk through frailejón and páramo zones
Return to Marulanda around 5:00 p.m.
experiences.tips: Altitude can affect some people — take the first day easy, stay well hydrated, and avoid alcohol the night before.

experiences.title: From Sheep to Loom: Centuries of Textile Tradition
experiences.subtitle: Shearing, spinning, dyeing, and hand weaving
experiences.description: Marulanda has been wool country since its founding. Visit sheep farms and learn the complete process: artisanal shearing, spinning on a drop spindle, dyeing with natural pigments, and weaving on a vertical loom. Hands-on workshops where you create your own piece.
experiences.longDescription: Marulanda's textile tradition spans more than two centuries. In these cold mountains, wool has always been warmth and sustenance: families raised sheep, sheared by hand, spun on drop spindles, and wove on vertical looms the ruanas, blankets, and ponchos still worn by muleteers today. This experience immerses you in that complete cycle. You start at the farm, meeting the sheep and watching a demonstration of artisanal shearing with scissors — just as the grandparents did. Then you move to the workshop, where you learn to wash the wool, card it, spin it on a drop spindle, and dye it with plants and minerals from the region. On the second day, you sit at a centuries-old vertical loom and weave your own piece under the guidance of a master artisan who inherited the craft from her mother and grandmother.
includes: Expert artisan guide in textile tradition
Complete materials (wool, spindle, loom, natural dyes)
Your own hand-woven piece to take home
Accommodation at a traditional farm (1 night)
itinerary: Day 1, morning: arrival at the farm with a warm welcome drink
Day 1, morning: visit to the sheepfold, artisanal shearing demonstration
Day 1, afternoon: workshop on washing, carding, and drop spindle spinning
Day 1, afternoon: natural dyeing workshop (plants, soil, bark)
Day 1, evening: dinner and bonfire with stories from muleteers and weavers
Day 2, morning: breakfast and introduction to the vertical loom
Day 2, morning and afternoon: guided weaving to create your own piece
Day 2, afternoon: piece delivery and experience wrap-up
whatToBring: Apron or clothes you can get dirty
Notebook to write down dye recipes
experiences.tips: No prior weaving experience needed. The master artisans guide you step by step with infinite patience. You'll leave with something made by your own hands, regardless of your skill level.

testimonialsData.quote: I thought the photos of the wax palms were exaggerated. They're not. It's another planet. And the people at La Ruta de la Lana make you feel at home from minute one.
testimonialsData.author: María Fernanda G.

testimonialsData.quote: I did the wool workshop with my teenage daughter. We both left knowing how to spin. When does that ever happen on a trip?
testimonialsData.author: Carlos M.

testimonialsData.quote: I'm a nature photographer and Marulanda left me speechless. The guides know exactly where and when the best light falls.
testimonialsData.author: James L.
